jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.punchline: Classic Homer catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Homer's signature catchphrase - iconic character moment but routine usage here

jokes.text: Marge, that's just a suggested car size.
jokes.setup: Marge points out a compact parking spot
jokes.punchline: Homer's twisted logic about parking restrictions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - absurd rationalization delivered with complete confidence

jokes.text: We're getting a lot of sparks here, Dad. / Uh-huh. Easy. Easy. Perfect. All right, everybody, out the window.
jokes.setup: Homer attempting to park, family giving directions
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual response to sparks and having family exit through window
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy escalates from sparks to window exit - good physical comedy buildup

jokes.text: Ernest Cuts the Cheese! Honey, I Hit a School Bus! Look Who's Oinking!
jokes.punchline: Absurd parody movie titles
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons parody titles - good wordplay but not character-specific

jokes.text: Why, Marge, you frisky little devil. [Sexy Growl]
jokes.punchline: Homer's inappropriate sexual interpretation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer misunderstanding - perfect character moment of inappropriate interpretation

jokes.text: Get me Jed Colic!
jokes.punchline: Absurd name parody in serious context
jokes.explanation: Typical Simpsons background gag - silly name in serious context

jokes.text: Oh, this movie's too complicated. / Hey, the floor's sticky.
jokes.punchline: Homer dismisses complexity while noticing gross theater details
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Homer's priorities (simple entertainment vs theater hygiene)

jokes.text: Who's that guy? What did that guy say when I said, 'Who's that guy?'
jokes.punchline: Meta-confusion about his own confusion
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation of confusion - Homer gets confused about his confusion, very characteristic

jokes.text: Look, you can see the strings. Ooh! An octopus!
jokes.punchline: Immediately distracted by simple visual
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - starts insightful critique then gets distracted by shiny object

jokes.text: If you don't watch the violence, you'll never get desensitized to it.
jokes.setup: Marge covering her eyes during violence
jokes.punchline: Homer's backwards parenting logic about desensitization
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - Homer presents terrible parenting advice as wisdom, highly quotable

jokes.text: It's pretty obvious if you think about it. / Shut up, Homer! No one wants to hear what you think! / Yeah!
jokes.setup: Homer spoiling movie ending with confidence
jokes.punchline: Crowd turns hostile with brutal putdown
jokes.explanation: Good cringe escalation - Homer's movie spoiling leads to public humiliation

jokes.text: Homer, if it makes you feel any better... most of what they threw at you splattered on me.
jokes.punchline: Marge's matter-of-fact description of being collateral damage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge deadpan delivery - supportive but honest about the mess

jokes.text: His tendons are throbbing. Stress is collecting in the trouble spots-- here, here, and here-- making for one unhappy pappy.
jokes.punchline: Clinical analysis ending with childish rhyme
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa character moment - sophisticated analysis with kid-like conclusion

jokes.text: Marge, I have always carried myself... with a certain quiet dignity. Tonight, you robbed me of it.
jokes.punchline: Claims of dignity after being loudly ejected from theater
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer delusion - claiming dignity after humiliating public outburst

jokes.text: I guess that executive stress ball we got him isn't working.
jokes.punchline: Marge's practical assessment of failed stress relief
jokes.explanation: Solid Marge understatement - practical response to emotional drama

jokes.text: Eww, a skunk.
jokes.punchline: Casual reaction to roadside skunk
jokes.explanation: Minor visual gag - sets rural scene but not particularly funny

jokes.text: Ooh, a redneck bar.
jokes.punchline: Homer's excited recognition of redneck establishment
jokes.explanation: Character moment showing Homer's attraction to lowbrow entertainment

jokes.text: Hey, you, let's fight. / Them's fightin' words.
jokes.setup: Challenge to fight at redneck bar
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of 'fighting words' cliché
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on cliché - circular logic of fighting words causing fight

jokes.text: We don't sell Duff. We sell Fudd. / Okay, Fudd me.
jokes.punchline: Accepts knockoff brand with unfortunate phrasing
jokes.explanation: Good brand parody with accidental innuendo from Homer's phrasing

jokes.text: It's a song I wrote while I was mopping up your dried blood and teeth.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of cleaning up violence aftermath
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor - casual reference to violent bar cleanup as song inspiration

jokes.text: That's right, except for the truck.
jokes.setup: Song about flipping pickup truck off interstate
jokes.punchline: Homer relates to everything except the specific truck detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response - agrees with emotional truth while nitpicking factual details

jokes.text: Your song touched me in a way I've never felt before. And which way to the can?
jokes.setup: Homer moved by emotional song performance
jokes.punchline: Immediately shifts to practical bathroom needs
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer misdirection - sincere emotion immediately undercut by basic needs

jokes.text: That's a pretty name. / You think so? / Maybe. I'm not sure. I forgot it.
jokes.punchline: Escalating uncertainty ending in complete memory failure
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Homer's indecisiveness - from compliment to complete mental blank

jokes.text: Let's end this feudin' and a-fussin' and get down to some lovin'.
jokes.setup: Homer returns home after country bar experience
jokes.punchline: Adopting country dialect and attitude
jokes.explanation: Character moment showing Homer easily influenced by new environment

jokes.text: Doctor, you weren't supposed to remove his gall bladder. Put it back! Put it back!
jokes.punchline: Absurd medical mistake and impossible solution
jokes.explanation: Background TV gag - absurd medical drama dialogue for atmosphere

jokes.text: Gee, Homer's singing to his ball again. / He's bowling a 280.
jokes.setup: Homer singing country song at bowling alley
jokes.punchline: Music improving his bowling performance
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to country influence - shows music's unexpected positive effect

jokes.text: Fudd? They took that off the market after all those hillbillies went blind.
jokes.setup: Homer asking for Fudd beer brand
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of product causing blindness
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor callback - explains why sketchy beer isn't available

jokes.text: Wait a minute. You went to another bar? / Moe, I was 100 miles out of town. / Oh, Homer.
jokes.setup: Moe discovers Homer went to different bar
jokes.punchline: Moe's hurt feelings despite distance excuse
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Moe's possessive bartender feelings despite reasonable explanation

jokes.text: 'I'm Basting a Turkey With My Tears'... 'Don't Look Up My Dress Unless You Mean It'... 'I'm Sick of Your Lying Lips and False Teeth.'
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific country song titles
jokes.explanation: Excellent country music parody - hilariously specific and emotionally overwrought titles

jokes.text: We've got to crack open your head and scoop out those songs.
jokes.setup: Homer wanting to record Lurleen's songs
jokes.punchline: Violently literal approach to music extraction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - solving recording problem with cartoon violence

jokes.text: This is KUDD 570 AM. Don't touch that dial. You've got KUDD on it.
jokes.punchline: Gross pun on station call letters
jokes.explanation: Standard radio pun - plays on station letters with mild gross-out humor

jokes.text: I thought I told you to stay away from my sister! / Oh, here's 50 bucks. Take her to the Copa.
jokes.punchline: Immediate turnaround to generous offer after music
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - shows power of country music to change emotions instantly

jokes.text: It's payback time. / Puts this whole riot into perspective, doesn't it?
jokes.punchline: Music making violence seem philosophical
jokes.explanation: Clever juxtaposition - mindless violence becoming thoughtful due to soundtrack

jokes.text: I could feel her country soul in every digitally encoded bit.
jokes.punchline: Mixing spiritual language with technical CD terminology
jokes.explanation: Good technical/emotional contrast - Homer mixing spiritual and digital language

jokes.text: Country music sucks. It takes precious air space from shock deejays... whose cruelty and profanity amuse us all.
jokes.punchline: Preferring shock jocks for their cruelty
jokes.explanation: Good Bart moment - sophisticated media criticism with antisocial preferences

jokes.text: like that jerk in the cowboy hat and that dead lady.
jokes.punchline: Dismissive descriptions of famous performers
jokes.explanation: Homer's crude way of referencing country stars without naming them

jokes.text: All I did was spend the afternoon in her trailer... watching her try on some outfits.
jokes.setup: Homer defending his relationship with Lurleen
jokes.punchline: Inadvertently making it sound more inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness - making innocent activity sound suspicious

jokes.text: I was gonna ask for some water, but now I feel guilty about it.
jokes.setup: Lurleen saying Homer doesn't want anything in return
jokes.punchline: Homer feeling guilty about basic human needs
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment - genuine guilt over innocent request due to praise

jokes.text: You're just a big sack of sugar. / Thanks. You did say 'sugar,' right?
jokes.punchline: Homer's uncertainty about whether he heard correctly
jokes.explanation: Homer's insecurity shows through - needs confirmation of positive assessment

jokes.text: I should warn you, I'm not great with figures. / That's okay. / I make stupid decisions. / Nobody's perfect. / I did bad in school. / I didn't even go.
jokes.punchline: Lurleen one-upping each deficiency with her own worse version
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure - each confession topped by worse admission

jokes.text: This is made from a space-age fabric... developed especially for Elvis. Sweat actually cleans this suit.
jokes.punchline: Impossible fabric properties for gross situation
jokes.explanation: Good absurd product description - fake science for disgusting feature

jokes.text: Your boyhood dream was to eat the world's biggest hoagie. You did it at the county fair last year, remember?
jokes.setup: Homer claiming manager dream is lifelong
jokes.punchline: Marge recalling his actual achieved dream
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - reveals Homer's actual simple dreams vs current fantasy

jokes.text: Buddy Holly stood here in 1958 and said, 'There is no way in hell I'll record in this dump.'
jokes.punchline: Historical rejection rather than endorsement
jokes.explanation: Subverts expectation of celebrity endorsement with famous rejection

jokes.text: Buddy Holly stood here in 1958 and said... 'There is no way in hell I'll record in this dump.'
jokes.punchline: Historical figure rejecting the venue
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - sets up prestigious history then reveals opposite

jokes.text: I thought you said she was overweight. / It takes two to lie-- one to lie and one to listen.
jokes.setup: Lurleen noting Homer's description was wrong
jokes.punchline: Producer's philosophical deflection about lying
jokes.explanation: Clever saying that sounds wise but doesn't actually address the question

jokes.text: Hey, Dad, can I do a hambone solo? / Stop it.
jokes.setup: Kid wanting to participate in recording session
jokes.punchline: Father's immediate shut-down
jokes.explanation: Quick family dynamic joke - overeager kid shut down by working parent

jokes.text: We're getting a grinding noise on the track. / You, off the bench.
jokes.punchline: Producer blaming his son for audio issue
jokes.explanation: Callback to kid's musical ambitions causing actual studio problems

jokes.text: Hey! Oh, you meant that as a compliment.
jokes.setup: Lurleen calling Homer smart and handsome
jokes.punchline: Homer's delayed realization it was positive
jokes.explanation: Homer's self-esteem issues showing - assumes negative intent first

jokes.text: Oh, that's hot. There isn't a man alive who wouldn't get turned on by that.
jokes.punchline: Completely missing that it's directed at him
jokes.explanation: Peak Homer obliviousness - praising seduction attempt without realizing it's aimed at him

jokes.text: while you and Lurleen were out judging that greased pig contest... Maggie got her first tooth.
jokes.setup: Marge confronting Homer about missing family time
jokes.punchline: Missed major milestone for absurd rural activity
jokes.explanation: Perfect guilt trip - important family moment vs ridiculous country fair activity

jokes.text: That's great, honey. Have you seen my rattlesnake hatband?
jokes.punchline: Homer's distracted non-response and country accessory focus
jokes.explanation: Devastating character moment - Homer completely dismissing family for costume concerns

jokes.text: As much as I hate that man right now... you got to love that suit.
jokes.punchline: Grudging admission about his appearance
jokes.explanation: Great Marge moment - maintaining perspective even while justifiably angry

jokes.text: They don't call me Colonel Homer because I'm some dumb-ass army guy.
jokes.setup: Record executive trying to buy Lurleen's contract
jokes.punchline: Homer's confident assertion while being exactly that naive
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic confidence - Homer asserting business savvy while being completely out of his depth

jokes.text: My whole romantic life is flashing before my eyes. / Gross!
jokes.setup: Homer about to be seduced by Lurleen
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sharing followed by son's disgusted reaction
jokes.explanation: Good family dynamic - Homer's oversharing met with appropriate child reaction

jokes.text: Just so I don't wonder--you would have gone all the way with me? / Uh-huh. / Ooh. Okay.
jokes.punchline: Homer's honest answer and Lurleen's satisfied reaction
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable honesty moment - both characters getting closure through direct communication

jokes.text: I have to tell you... I'm desperate to unload Lurleen and I'll take any offer. / I'll give you 50 bucks. / You son of a-- Sold.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to negotiate from position of strength
jokes.punchline: Immediately accepting terrible lowball offer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer business incompetence - reveals desperation then takes insulting offer

jokes.text: I caught my wife in bed... with my best friend. / You bitter? / Yep. Bit him too.
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of 'bitter' leading to violence confession
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay escalation - emotional pain becomes literal biting

jokes.text: Is there any room in that bed for a dad-burned fool? / Always has been.
jokes.setup: Homer's humble return after affair temptation
jokes.punchline: Marge's loving acceptance with gentle dig
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character moment - loving reconciliation with perfect callback to Homer's foolishness

jokes.text: I do.
jokes.setup: Lurleen's song about Marge being lucky
jokes.punchline: Marge's simple, heartfelt agreement
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending moment - Marge's recognition of her fortune despite Homer's flaws
